blocks.text: Brackenridge Civil Group is a civil engineering practice organized to design the parts of a project that are buried, graded, paved, or drained. The firm works for municipal public works departments, for water and sewer authorities, and for private development clients, and it takes a project from preliminary layout through permitting, bidding, and construction administration. Drawings issued from this office go out under a sealed cover sheet. The engineer whose seal appears on that sheet has read every drawing behind it.

The practice is small by intention. There is no marketing department and no separate project management staff. The engineer who attends the progress meeting is the engineer who prepared the plan under discussion, and the client who telephones this office is put through to that person rather than to an account representative. The firm has grown by keeping its clients rather than by adding new ones quickly, and it does not submit a proposal for work it is not prepared to staff.

items: Site grading and earthwork. Grading plans, cut and fill analysis, retaining structure coordination, and erosion and sedimentation control plans and narratives.

Stormwater management. Drainage area studies, runoff calculations, detention and retention basin design, conveyance and inlet sizing, outlet protection, and the reports required to carry a design through county and state review.

Roadway design. Horizontal and vertical geometry, pavement sections, sight distance and intersection analysis, curb and sidewalk details, signing and pavement marking plans, and maintenance of traffic.

Utility design. Water distribution and sanitary sewer layout and profiles, service connections, pump station and meter vault coordination, and adjustment of existing facilities in conflict with proposed work.
Surveying and base mapping coordination, including establishment of horizontal and vertical control and preparation of existing conditions plans from field survey.

Construction phase services. Shop drawing and submittal review, response to requests for information, periodic observation of the work, review of contractor payment applications, punch list preparation, and record drawings.

blocks.text: The firm does not perform structural design of buildings, geotechnical investigation, or environmental site assessment. Where a project requires those services, the firm says so at the proposal stage and coordinates with a qualified consultant of the client's choosing rather than representing the work as its own.
The firm's engagements have been with the following clients:

These engagements have a common shape. The firm is brought in early enough to have an opinion about where the water goes, prepares the plans and the supporting calculations, sits through the review comments, and stays on the job while it is built. The work for {{fact:f:E-2001-001.client}} and for {{fact:f:E-2005-001.client}} was drawn on the understanding that a plan is inspected against the linework and then maintained for thirty years by a crew that did not attend the design meetings; the firm draws accordingly, using a standard detail where a standard detail will serve and a note on the plan wherever the intent is not obvious. The work for {{fact:f:E-2004-001.client}} and for {{fact:f:E-2006-001.client}} was prepared to the same standard on the calendar the review schedule allowed, the firm's practice being to identify the approval that will govern the date and work backward from it.

Repeat work is the firm's principal measure of its own performance, and the firm's more recent commissions, among them its work for {{fact:f:E-2007-001.client}}, have come to this office on the strength of plans it had already delivered elsewhere. References are available on request. The firm will provide the name of the person who administered the contract rather than the name of the person most likely to be complimentary.

Every project receives a lettered project folder at the time the agreement is executed. The folder holds the signed agreement, all correspondence in and out, minutes of every progress meeting, the design calculations, and the review comments with the firm's written response to each. Nothing is agreed to by telephone and left there; a call that changes a scope or a schedule is confirmed in writing the same week. The firm's invoices state the work performed in the period against the tasks named in the agreement, and the firm does not invoice for work outside the agreement without written authorization obtained beforehand.

Inquiries regarding the firm's qualifications, availability, or fee for a particular project may be directed to Mark Todd, Principal Engineer. The firm will contact the requesting party within one week of receiving a request for proposal and will state plainly whether it intends to submit. Mark Todd signs each of the firm's agreements and each letter issued to a public body, and correspondence on any active project may be addressed to him.
